posts.title: Week 1: Aarav Builds Wallet Onboarding
posts.intro: Aarav is a 22-year-old frontend developer shifting into Web3 through the Stellar track.
posts.targetProject: A beginner wallet onboarding flow for first-time users joining campus blockchain clubs.
posts.why: New users drop off before making the first transaction because setup feels unclear.
posts.how: Step-by-step flow mapping, one click per action, and transaction confirmation states.
posts.life: He balances this build with final semester exams and 2 hours of coding every night.

posts.title: Week 3: Meera Starts Event Tracking Dashboard
posts.intro: Meera is a product-minded engineer moving from Web2 analytics to on-chain product tooling.
posts.targetProject: A contract event dashboard that lets teams monitor key user actions in near real time.
posts.why: Early teams cannot improve products if user behavior is hidden inside raw logs.
posts.how: Indexed events, retry-safe polling, and a simple operator view for daily checks.
posts.life: She recently switched jobs and still ships one stable module each weekend.

posts.title: Week 6: Rohan Pushes Mini dApp to Real Users
posts.intro: Rohan is a self-taught full-stack builder focused on practical utility products.
posts.targetProject: A mini dApp for freelance milestone payments with transparent release conditions.
posts.why: Small teams lose trust when payment status and delivery terms are not visible.
posts.how: Contract-first design, strict edge-case testing, and user validation every Friday.
posts.life: He freelances full-time and works on this project before client calls every morning.
